page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton processor utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, this server features 8 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results position the c8gd.2xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1760.0 and a multi-core score of 9615.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 7668.87. Memory bandwidth tests show 924.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 162.67 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the weak category at 1512.16. For language model tasks, the instance handles text generation for a 7B model at an average speed of 11.3 tokens/sec, though prompt processing for the same model is poor at 15.56 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gd.2xlarge offers a balanced profile by combining dedicated ARM64 compute cores with high-speed local NVMe storage on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. This hardware density makes it highly suitable for workloads that require a mix of compute power and fast local scratch space. Recommended workloads include static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 19.42 GB/sec, database operations, and caching layers like Redis, which registered 16.71 million operations per second in benchmark testing.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server delivers average performance across standard compute benchmarks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 9615.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 7668.87. While cryptographic performance is relatively weak, the instance excels in memory bandwidth and local storage access. This hardware profile makes the c8gd.2xlarge highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated ARM compute paired with fast local storage, such as database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and general-purpose utility computing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.2xlarge features 8 dedicated ARM64 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for database and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture.
16.0 GB of system memory paired with a 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store.
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Strong database performance with 16.71 million Redis SET operations per second.
Well-suited for static web serving, database operations, and caching workloads.
